title: 4-7-8 Breath: Four Rounds
subtitle: A breath practice for slowing the nervous system before sleep
excerpt: A five-minute breath practice for Day 9 of the sleep arc. Inhale for four counts, hold for seven, exhale for eight. Four rounds. The extended exhale activates the parasympathetic nervous system, the pattern the body needs to move from alert to rest.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Built from the Day 9 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795): soothing the body with breath. The 4-7-8 breath is Andrew Weil's adaptation of pranayama breathing techniques from yoga traditions.

content.text: A breath practice for Day 9 of the sleep arc. Four rounds of 4-7-8 counting. The extended exhale is the active ingredient. It signals to the nervous system that the emergency is over and rest is available.
New to body-based meditation? Read
body-based meditation
first.
The practice
Setting up
Sit up straight or lie down, either works. Tongue resting gently on the ridge behind your upper front teeth. This position is part of Andrew Weil's specific technique; it helps regulate the exhale.
The meditation
One complete round:
Exhale completely through your mouth with a whoosh sound.
Close your mouth. Inhale quietly through your nose for a count of four.
Hold for a count of seven.
Exhale completely through your mouth with a whoosh for a count of eight.
That's one round. Do four rounds without hurrying between them. If the counts feel too long at first, use a faster tempo, what matters is the ratio (4:7:8), not the absolute speed.
Coming back
After four rounds, breathe normally. Notice whether anything has shifted. Some people feel lightheaded the first few times, that settles with practice. Lie down if you haven't already.
Where this practice comes from
The 4-7-8 breath is Andrew Weil's adaptation of pranayama breathing techniques from yoga traditions. Built here from the Day 9 theme of
SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive
(Rebecca J Page, 2025): soothing the body with breath.
